name: Mixed Vegetable Pulao
recipeIngredient: 1 small green pepper, cut into 2.5 cm long strips (25 g.)
50 grams carrots, scraped and sliced fine
140 grams basmati rice, washed and soaked in cold water for 30 minutes and drained
25 grams frozen green peas or fresh green peas, boiled until almost tender
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the ghee or butter over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic. Fry for eight minutes or until golden brown.
2. Add the ground spices (black cumin seeds, coriander seeds, black peppercorns, bay leaf, red chili, and cinnamon) to the mixture. Stir frequently.
3. Add the turmeric powder and stir for two minutes over low heat.
4. Add the cauliflower (or mushrooms), green pepper, and carrots. Stir-fry for two to three minutes.
5. Mix in the rice and fry for an additional two to three minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Stir in the green peas, sweet corn, and salt. Cook well until all ingredients are combined.
7. Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
8. Cover the pan and simmer until all the water has been absorbed (12 to 15 minutes). Longer cooking times may be needed for other types of long grain rice.
9. Do not lift the lid or stir the rice during cooking.
10. After boiling, remove the pan from the heat. Let it sit uncovered for two minutes so steam can escape.
11. Cover again and allow the dish to rest undisturbed for 10 minutes before serving.
12. Optionally, substitute cauliflower with button mushrooms (125 g., halved or quartered) for variety. Serve with barrah kababs, tandoori chicken, nargisi kababs, and a raita or chutney.
